title: Watch: Jayson Tatum receives thunderous ovation in first game back with Celtics
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics lineup Friday after a 297-day recovery from a ruptured Achilles tendon.
Tatum played six minutes, recording two assists and two rebounds in his first game back against the Dallas Mavericks.
The Celtics, second in the Eastern Conference with a 41-21 record, anticipate Tatum's impact for their playoff push.

title: Report: Patriots to trade C Garrett Bradbury to Bears
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots to trade center Garrett Bradbury to Bears for a fifth-round draft pick, per ESPN's Adam Schefter.
The trade cannot be finalized until the new NFL league year commences next week.
Trade frees $5.7 million in cap space, with Patriots eyeing center replacement, potentially from within.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Hamnet', a fictional tale related to Shakespeare's son, is now streaming on Peacock.
Jafar Panahi's 'It Was Just An Accident' critiques Iranian rule; available on Hulu.
Netflix will lose 'Arrested Development' streaming rights on March 15.

title: What Joe Mazzulla said about Jayson Tatum’s return to Celtics starting lineup
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to the Celtics' starting lineup for the first time this season, facing the Dallas Mavericks.
Celtics coach Joe Mazzulla praised Tatum's work ethic and communication throughout his recovery process.
Tatum replaces Baylor Scheierman in the lineup without any specified minutes restriction for his comeback.

title: Bruins trade for former first-round pick Lukas Reichel
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston Bruins traded a 2026 sixth-round pick to Vancouver for forward Lukas Reichel.
Reichel, 23, was the 17th overall pick in the 2020 NHL Draft, scoring 22 goals in 188 NHL games.
Reichel will start in Providence after scoring seven points in 19 NHL games this season.

title: 2 Boston restaurants to pay  $400K for failure to pay service fees to staff, AGO alleges
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Two Boston restaurants, Carrie Nation Cocktail Club and The Dubliner, ordered to pay over $422,000 in restitution and penalties.
AGO alleged the restaurants mislabeled a 3% fee intended for service staff from May 2023 to June 2024.
East Coast Tavern Group cited a point-of-sale system error, asserting fee intended to cover rising post-COVID-19 costs.

title: Patriots reportedly had this trade offer for A.J. Brown rejected by Eagles
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Patriots' offer for A.J. Brown included a first-round and third-round pick, but the Eagles declined.
Philadelphia seeks a first-round pick with a second-round sweetener for A.J. Brown, per NFL insider Mike Garafolo.
Trade discussions are ongoing, with potential moves expected before the NFL free agency period begins March 11.

title: Connecticut woman running 2026 Boston Marathon after Alzheimer’s strikes her family again
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Colleen Graham from Norwalk, Connecticut, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise awareness for Alzheimer's.
Graham's father, a retired surgeon and former marathon runner, was diagnosed with Alzheimer's in 2024.
She aims to raise $30,000 for Alzheimer's research in hopes of finding a cure for future patients.

title: New England travel tips for the last days of winter
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: The South Boston St. Patrick's Day Parade on March 15 is projected to draw up to 1 million visitors.
The 43rd Annual Maine Maple Sunday weekend will occur March 21-22, featuring events at various sugarhouses and farms.
Halfinger Farms in Higganum, Conn., will reopen April 1 with over 150,000 daffodils available for picking.

title: Fundraiser launched for family of Southborough crash victim, beloved local bartender
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Crystal Johnson-Clementi died in a Southborough car crash; a GoFundMe for her family raised $46,000, close to its goal.
Johnson-Clementi, a bartender at Horseshoe Pub & Restaurant, survived breast cancer before her passing at age 37.
She is survived by her husband, son, two sisters, two brothers, two nieces, and three nephews.
